rationale.R1.1: The section makes an explicit contribution claim ('first O(1/√T) rate') and frames a thesis about selection vs. synthesis; reconstructing and validating this framing is necessary for any substantive review.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the section contains multiple internally testable mathematical assertions—O(1/√T) in the abstract vs. O(log K/√T) later, L-smoothness and μ-strong-convexity invoked on an object described as non-convex, and iteration complexity claims—each of which can invalidate the contribution if inconsistent.
rationale.R1.3: Empirical backing is punted to 'Section 5' without evidence in-section; cited rates (Cesa-Bianchi & Lugosi, Bubeck) are invoked as upper-bound matches whose accuracy must be checked.
rationale.R1.4: The word 'first' appears explicitly; the novelty claim must be assessed against the cited prior work and the stated regime (bandit-style feedback, hard-selection objective).
rationale.R1.7: Two specific references (Cesa-Bianchi & Lugosi 2006; Bubeck 2015) are used as anchors for rate comparisons; citation accuracy and completeness affect whether the novelty and consistency claims hold.
